name: Pork and Pepper Stew with Oranges
recipeIngredient: 3 1/2 Pounds boneless pork shoulder or butt, trimmed
2 Pounds onions, halved and thickly sliced
1 Cup celery, cut into half-inch slices
3 Medium poblano chiles, seeded and thinly sliced
1 medium red bell pepper, thinly sliced
2 cups canned tomatoes in juice, diced
2 tablespoons California or New Mexico chile powder
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the pork generously with salt and pepper.
2. In a heavy casserole or Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat. Brown the pork in batches if necessary; remove and set aside.
3. Add the remaining olive oil to the pot. Sauté onions, garlic, celery, poblanos, jalapeno, and red pepper over moderate heat until just beginning to color.
4. Stir in fennel seed, oregano, tomatoes, stock, white wine, chile powder, and shrimp powder. Bring mixture to a boil.
5. Return pork to the pot with raisins; reduce heat and cover. Simmer gently for about 35-40 minutes or until pork is tender.
6. Remove from heat and skim any fat from the surface.
7. Stir in chopped cilantro just before serving.
8. Serve with Orange-Cinnamon Rice and garnish with orange segments and cilantro sprigs.
Yield: 6 to 8 servings
